Razor Sharp Short Sword  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 26
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +7 DEX: +7
WT: 4.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Razor Sharp Short Sword
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 26
DMG: 16 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +7 DEX: +7
WT: 4.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Slashing
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Biggins Black
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-30 13:49:45
Page Updated:Thu May 19th, 2011

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Veeshan's Peak 1.0
NPC Name
Milyex Vioren
Xygoz


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Veeshan's Peak 1.0

Modern UI inspect window - Razor Sharp Short Sword

Uploaded October 26th, 2020 by Andhrimnir
Send a correction
Post Comment
Drop info - Razor Sharp Short Sword
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2020 at 4:24 PM Rating: Good
Andhrimnir
Guru
251 posts
Confirmed to drop from Xygoz during Kunark era on TLP. It may move in the revamp for VP 2.0 during OoW/GoD release. Also note that the weapon does NOT require level 40, in the screenshot of the modern UI window that I uploaded someone had placed an augment into it which requires level 40.

Edited, Oct 26th 2020 5:42pm by Andhrimnir

Caps
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2001 at 11:04 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
And at level 60, piercing, 1hs, and 1hb all cap the same, someone mentioned earlier that piercing caps higher...it doesn't, not at 60 anyway.
▲ Page top
Agi comment
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 12:37 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763474247
21 posts
Rahzzin's comments on how weapon delay works are an incorrect - an old speculation that I
believed had been dispelled with the rummors that dex increased attack speed and agi increased run speed. At any rate the offhand delay matters as much as primary hand delay. - BADDUDE

Well, BADDUDE, the rest of your reasoning is certainly called into question when you consider that your comment on agi and run speed is 100% WRONG. Agi DEFINITELY increases run speed. Why do you think that the +2 Agi rings dropped by Nillipuss are called Rings of Running? DUH! As a level 60 Druid it always amazes me that when I SoW another class they OUTRUN me. The reason I ALWAYS find out is that they have more Agi than me. Before you post perhaps you should spend a few seconds doing research so that yo dont misinform people.
▲ Page top
RE: Agi comment
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2001 at 10:58 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
1) Offhand delay does matter.

2) Agi does not affect running speed, get 255 agi and you aren't running any faster. They're probably out running you because they strafe run, strafing while running is faster than running in a straight line.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 10 2001 at 3:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Agi does affect running speed.
RE: a little about AGI (dealt with in Oasis of Marr)
QuoteReply
# Aug 11 2001 at 6:25 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764994717
10 posts
Ahh, the blazing sun...so hot..beating down on me relentlessly...but I don't worry. I've got food and water and I'm walking at a fair speed. A good lvl later, I've got enough garbage on me to slow down Rallos Zek himself. (and yea, that WAS a joke) I cash it all in to the local vendors and promptly loot some more. unfortunately, while trying to lvl, I bit off more than I could chew, and a Gator chewed on me...
I was fortunate enough to have someone witness my untimly circus act gone bad, and they rezzed me. now, aside from rez side effects and my blue bar being nonexist, I seemed alright. I sat and meditated and thought happy thoughts on the dock. the cool ocean breeze felt good. I stood up and saw a few abandon corpses laying around and I looted them for cash upon asking who they belonged to, with no reply.
I noticed that my AGI was still very much in the red, and the rez effect had already expired...my health and vigor were immaculate, yet my land speed suffererd severly...what was wrong? I had no loot on me to sell off..I had already sold it all........for...money...*sigh* I had traded one weight for another, and was suffering the weight of roughly 600-700 copper, 500 or so silver, 300 or 400 gold and a about 10 plat.....I wearily trudge through the desert to the nearest bank... My Selo's jamming on a Mistmoore Battle drum hardly made up...and the Anthem gave me enough STR to move. I was virtual bait for anything that would have its way with me.
I eventually found a nice chap who offered me a port to the Rivervale bank to cash my woes in for a lighter load, and to make a sale or two. I agreed, and upon loading the cash into the bank (and into his pocket) I walked away with two lambent bracers, lambent gaunts and my AGI at it's best. I strapped on my drum and jammed Selo's which took me home on no time. I felt if I had just spread my arms (not legs) I could fly!...but that makes playing the drum a little more difficult...but...that's how I feel that AGI most certianly affects land speed. argue THAT!
P.S. I really like this wep, and would like one. anyone wanna travel the world with me and pick it up? i'm on Dru Ro. :) *wink*
▲ Page top
RE: Agi comment
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2001 at 11:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249706114
7 posts
It's difficult to tell if this is supposed to be a joke, COUNTESSKRAK!
▲ Page top
Offhanded remarks
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 11:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249461149
62 posts
The only piercer I kept in the offhand was the Walrus Tooth when I was on a HP kick. This was only for 5 or so levels around the 20s. The rest of the time it was a 1hs or 1hb and my piercing is still maxed for my level, while the 1hs is maybe 5-10 points lower. The higher damage or a decent effect more than makes up for not having a second piercer. Anyhow the thing I wanted to mention is; The Double Attack works on the offhand, so keep that in your calcualtions when figuring out your ******** The Tooth would hit for 1-10 points and in doubles. The 1hs I have hits for 20-30 and often in doubles. For us Rogues, the faster that mob goes down, the better off we are, especially while soloing (snort-snicker)
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 07 2001 at 12:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) friend and i will be powerleveling peopel on sol ro from 1-50 in about a weeks time emiaiil em at laskar5@excite.com
Not best weapon in the game :)
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 6:56 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249493556
64 posts
Saw a pic of a 16/24 1h blunt weapon (monk only) that was an accidental drop in lfay =)
▲ Page top
Offhand delay DOES matter.
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 2:59 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249706114
7 posts
The attack delay of the weapons is calculated seperately when you use a weapon in both hands. The dual wield skill is used to determine if, when the offhand weapon's delay has expired, an attack attempt will be made with that weapon.

Rahzzin's comments on how weapon delay works are an incorrect - an old speculation that I believed had been dispelled with the rummors that dex increased attack speed and agi increased run speed. At any rate the offhand delay matters as much as primary hand delay.
▲ Page top
RE: Offhand delay DOES matter.
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 3:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591752664645
Scholar
64 posts
Dual wield skill means everything.
At low levels delay in offhand will mean mostly nothing. Your chance of actually swinging the offhand is pathetic, so having high damage is great. At higher level when dual wield gets close to 200, try equipping a 30 delay weapon and then a 20 delay in offhand and see the difference on logs. And I don't believe "dual wield" skill is cycled on offhand delay, its by primary, the faster your primary hand the more your offhand swings too it seems on parsing(between punching and 19 delay weapon using same offhand)
▲ Page top
RE: Offhand delay DOES matter.
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2001 at 7:48 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Dual wield skill is definitely dictated by the delay of the OFFHAND weapon. If it weren't, there would be no way that my warrior could swing his 18 delay offhand weapon 4 times before his primary (a 25/40 wurmslayer) cycles. Going by your theory, any offhand weapon I use would be downgraded to a 40 delay to match my wurmslayer, and that simply isn't the case. Also, if it were dictated by main hand delay, wouldn't everyone's offhand weapon swing at exactly the same time as the main hand if the main hand weapon was slower? That certainly isn't the case either. I've used a wurmslayer for over 20 levels, and my offhand weapons all work on their own separate delay.
▲ Page top
Bad to have a quick offhand on lower levels?!
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2001 at 12:36 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249706114
7 posts
This is REALY false, the speed of attempts will determine how fast your skill increases, and for some classes the dual wield skill is not capped at the same level as the normal 5per level + 10, so getting that skill up is, at first, paramount.
▲ Page top
RE: Idea..
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 4:47 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591757464810
156 posts
Can you put some non-weapon item like a torch in your primary hand while dual weilding to just test out your secondary weapon rate and damage? It would be easier that way than trying to filter out the main weapon hand damage.
▲ Page top
RE: Idea..
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2001 at 12:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249706114
7 posts
If this doesn't work you could use a piercer in one hand and a slash or blunt weapon in the other and only measure the off hand's attack rate.
▲ Page top
RE: Offhand delay DOES matter.
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 8:04 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591755936927
73 posts
This is correct, in that offhand delay does in fact matter as to when your second weapon attacks, BUT since you do not get a damage bonus to your offhand weapon, most folks prefer a weapon with a little more oomph to it. At higher levels, a lower delay is a bit more preferable for most melees for their main hand weapon than a bit extra base damage, cause you can be doing 10, 15 bonus damage per swing twice as often than 2 or 4 more base damage half as often. This does not apply to secondary melee slot. Hence the reason the Mosscovered Twig went from Uber to Crap in one swell foop when they made it offhand only.
▲ Page top
On the other hand.
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2001 at 12:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249706114
7 posts
This is true, but it only means that the offhand weapon's damage is based exclusivly on it's ratio, therefore a higher damage weapon has NO partucular advantage.

Its not piercing cause this would be too powerful this was made when there were no epics...and even now the best piercer is the primal velium from sleepers at 16/24.

I havn't talked to anyone that owns one of these(primal velium) nor do I think one exists no ayonae ro yet, but I would imagine with duelist one could backstab for well over 1400.

an EXELENT offhand weapon none the less...

59 rogue of ayonae ro

Yes, this dmg/dly ratio isn't too shabby.

__DEL__1591755689526

Are you saying that everyone being the same is a good thing and you don't like variety or being unique? Then you are in the minority, my friend.

SHadow Epic
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 9:29 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757592958
73 posts
I've seen one, they look cool, unlike the rogue one. The rogue one looks like it has a graphics glitch, not particle effects :(
▲ Page top
maybe it's just me but
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 5:40 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I could be mistaken cause i am just starting to get to the point of not being a lowbie, at level 40, but from what i have seen, most of us rogues seem to be two piercing weapons, by the time we are of sufficient level to earn an item like this. It's a great weapon as far as stats/delay, and I may have to pick up a gnoll hide lariat again to bring 1hs up to par, as I haven't used it in at least 15 levels i would say. But you really wonder what verant is thinking at times when they make class only weapons that seem against the grain for the class. They have been doing that alot with Velious I have noticed. 2h-Piercing for pally/shadowknights, when most seem to go with the 1hs/1hb and a shield route, both for the ac, and for the interupts.

It's not a bad weapon, and what rogue wouldn't like more strength for upping their max backstab, it just seems against the norm IMHO
▲ Page top
RE: maybe it's just me but
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 7:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756368265
66 posts
Probably the main reason to not use a piercing weapon in off hand is the fact that high damage piercing weapons are very hard to acquire. Obviously, this is due to how backstab damage is calculated.

The highest damage rogue useable piercing weapon available in the game has a damage of 15. If you can finda 1HS or 1HB with a better damage and comparable delay, go ahead and put it into the off hand. You're not going to be backstabbing with that. It makes it even better if you can find one that procs something useful or has decent stats on it. All in all, it really doesn't matter _that_ much whether you use piercing, 1HS, or 1HB in off hand...just use the best weapon you can find there. Yes, I know that for rogue, piercing skil maxes higher than either of the others, but I'm really not sure if it makes so much difference that you should sacrifice a higher dmg other type weapon to use a piercer because you have a higher piercing skill.

Any other ideas?
▲ Page top
RE: maybe it's just me but
QuoteReply
# Jul 05 2001 at 5:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756015544
Scholar
20 posts
Many rogues use a 1hs or 1hb in the offhand. There are many good weapons in those classes out there that are easier/cheaper to get than an equivelant piercer. My advice would be to get both 1hb and 1hs up to max. You may ci\onsider a Blackjack for the blunt...7/21 and reletavily cheap at 300pp or so.
▲ Page top
RE: maybe it's just me but
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 12:42 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761912254
135 posts
True, but the Blackjack is still not cheap or easy to obtain. Besides, you want as high damage as possible in the offhand, and hell, its not hard to get a weapon with higher damage than a Blackjack. Cold Iron Morning Star, which is almost worthless these days, is still 9dam. I believe the Forged Morning Stars are also 9dam. Giant Militia Longswords are 11dam, and though I dunno where they drop, I sometimes see them on vendors and sell for only 5pp or so. Since the delay doesn't matter in the offhand, go as high damage as possible, but yes, I agree Ildera, you are better off as a rogue keeping 1hs and 1hb high for the offhand, as well as Piercing maxed for your primary hand.
▲ Page top
just thought i'd inform you...
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2001 at 10:45 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249322476
13 posts
The Barbarian Shamans are called "The Shamen of Justice", not the "Shamen of the North".
